purpose: Held-out ACCEPTANCE GATE for LOINC search ranking/recall. Derived from a grounded lab answer key (expected_loinc is human-grounded against loinc.fractal.ly /api/loinc, not model output). Use this frozen: tune coefficients on an independently-labeled curated set, freeze, then run this once. Do NOT fit the common_test_rank weight (or any weight) to this file, and never derive labels from a feature that is in the model.
qa: Labels spot-checked against live loinc.fractal.ly data. 4 fixes applied (iron-saturation name/unit conflict; RDW-% rank-dependent + reject; Cells/uL dimension-not-magnitude; RDW-SD alt tightened).
usage_notes: query = analyte name as a lab prints it; unit = printed unit (UCUM-ish, caret 10^N notation preserved on purpose so the unit-normalization fix is exercised).
expected_loinc = the grounded canonical; alt = other acceptable equivalents from the key; a row passes if the chosen code is expected_loinc or in alt.

reject (optional) = codes that must NOT outrank expected_loinc; a row fails if any reject code ranks at or above expected_loinc, even if expected_loinc is also present. Used where a same-dimension attractor caused a documented miss.
controls tag each row's eval role (see control_legend). Rows with no special role have controls: [].
This file is breadth-limited (one real lab, 57 rows) and treatment-heavy. It is a regression/acceptance guard, NOT a tuning objective.

MISSING by construction: rare/unranked-canonical collision controls (B-12-class, specialty markers whose canonical has common_test_rank=0) and legitimately-qualitative web-UI rows. Those are the highest-value controls and must be labeled independently (lab eval or human curation) - not guessed. A few collision rows that happen to appear in this lab (PSA, A/G ratio, the WBC/RBC lay names) are flagged below as a starting point.
unit_assumed rows: the 3 urine markers were not in the probe extraction this run, so their printed units are best-effort standard notation, not observed.

name_unit_conflict rows: score NAME-RECALL ONLY (ignore unit). A correct dimension gate will legitimately drop the expected code under the printed unit, so unit-gating these rows produces false failures.

control_legend.collision: abbreviation or lay name where lexical search was OBSERVED to surface wrong variants over the canonical
control_legend.abbreviation: name carries an abbreviation (collision risk plausible but not verified here)
control_legend.unit_dependent: the correct code depends on the printed unit (count vs %, CV vs SD, ratio vs absolute)
control_legend.judgment: answer key flags a policy/variant call; acceptance may depend on a chosen convention
control_legend.search_ok_model_miss: search already ranks the canonical at or near #1; any miss here is downstream model selection, not search - do not 'fix' in search
control_legend.unit_assumed: printed unit is best-effort (marker not present in the source extraction run)
control_legend.name_unit_conflict: printed unit's dimension contradicts the expected code's dimension (verified: expected is a fraction/%, printed unit is mass/volume). Score NAME-RECALL ONLY - do not apply the unit gate to this row; a correct dimension gate would correctly drop the expected code under the printed unit.
control_legend.rank_dependent: expected code and a known wrong attractor are indistinguishable on property, scale, and units; only common_test_rank separates them. Passes only after the rank signal is in place; never add the attractor to alt - see reject.
control_legend.dimension_not_magnitude: printed unit shares the canonical's dimension but differs in magnitude (e.g. Cells/uL ~ /uL vs canonical 10*3/uL). Must pass via the dimension-CLASS gate, NOT strict-magnitude unit equality; strict magnitude matching would wrongly drop the expected code.

rows.note: canonical component is Leukocytes; lay name surfaces 'WBC Other' variants
absolute count; percent counterpart is 736-9. Cells/uL is dimension-equal but magnitude-off vs canonical 10*3/uL
canonical is automated erythrocyte count; method-unspecified 26453-1 tends to out-rank it
/100 WBC ratio = 58413-6; exact-string unit match drops it for the fetal 62245-6
RDW-CV; percent selects 788-0. 30385-9 (also Erythrocyte/DistWidth/%) is indistinguishable except by common_test_rank and was the original RBC->RDW miss - must NOT outrank 788-0, never add to alt
canonical 17849-1 already ranks #1 in search; downstream model picked 4679-7
specific gravity is unitless; empty unit = no unit gate, scores name-recall
pH is unitless; empty unit = no unit gate, scores name-recall
MDRD non-black (48642-3) vs CKD-EPI 2021 race-free (98979-8); acceptance depends on chosen convention
albumin/globulin ratio; 'a g ratio' tokens collide with X/Creatinine ratios; 1751-7 (albumin) is the wrong attractor
downstream model abstained (returned no code); search recall TBD
total PSA; bare query surfaces free/complexed variants, total is ~rank 34 without the unit hint

2502-3 is MFr/% (a fraction); printed unit ug/dL is mass/volume - dimensions conflict. SCORE NAME-RECALL ONLY. Operator: confirm whether the lab actually prints '%' (then set unit to '%') or is mislabeling serum iron (2498-4)
RDW-SD; fL/EntVol selects 21000-5 (the %/DistWidth CV code 788-0 is the paired row above). Strict: do not accept 788-0 here, that would defeat the unit-driven discrimination
